penalties.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out and freed from the gauze by the end of the visible footage.

consensus_score.reasoning: Both models agreed on timing (106 seconds). Critical disagreement on task completion: Claude reported incomplete circle (automatic zero), while GPT reported successful completion with minor line deviation. Given the fundamental nature of task completion as a binary requirement in the rubric, the incomplete_circle penalty takes precedence and results in zero score.

claude_value.description: The marked circle was not completely cut out and freed from the gauze by the end of the visible footage.

gpt_value.description: Slight deviation occurred near the top right section of the circle.

disagreements.rationale: Task completion is a binary requirement in the rubric. If the circle was not freed from the gauze, this constitutes task failure regardless of cutting accuracy. The incomplete_circle penalty results in automatic zero per rubric rules.

disagreements.resolution: Used Claude's phase list without circle_release
disagreements.rationale: If the circle was not successfully freed (incomplete_circle penalty), then circle_release phase did not occur. Phase detection must be consistent with completion status.
Incomplete_circle penalty results in automatic zero score per rubric requirements, regardless of time performance or minor deviations.
